_doc: Oracle for tests/test-task-spec-skill.sh --suite fixtures. Each entry maps a fixture filename to the expected validate-task-spec.sh outcome.

_schema.expected_match: substring that MUST appear in the validate output (case-sensitive)

fixtures.description: Variant 4: grep -c ... ; echo $? captures exit, not match count

Variant 5: multi-line non-integer substitution — runtime check (--dry-run-eval) detects this, not the static lint. Static validate passes; oracle declares expected_exit=0 because we are testing the static lint here, and the --dry-run-eval suite is a separate scope.
Variant 6: $(cmd) || true followed by numeric compare without ${var:-0} normalisation
signed_off:true with signed_off_by=(none) and signed_off_at=(none) — structural sign-off envelope check rejects
Canonical clean v2.1 spec — validates cleanly with no errors. Uses `! grep -q PATTERN file` (the recommended replacement for inverted-grep-c).

v2.1.1 Bug 1 regression: signed_off:true but signed_off_by/signed_off_at lines absent entirely. Pre-fix, the pipefail-broken grep|awk|xargs silently aborted the validator with 0 bytes output. Post-fix, the envelope error must fire.
v2.1.1 Bug 1 regression: legacy spec with no signed_off: line at all. Envelope check must NOT fire (signed_off defaults to false when the line is absent).
WS-B Variant 7: backtick form of Variant 1 — `grep -c X file || echo 0`. The per-command regex only matches $(...) so this is caught by the v2.1.1 umbrella rule.
WS-B: $(awk '/PAT/{n++} END{print n+0}' file || echo 0) — awk isn't grep -c or wc, so only the umbrella rule catches it.
WS-B: $(python3 -c '...' 2>/dev/null || echo 0) — python isn't in any per-command fast-path, so only the umbrella rule catches it.
WS-B: $(jq '. | length' < x || echo 0) — jq isn't in any per-command fast-path, so only the umbrella rule catches it.

WS-C / v2.1.1 honest-limitation fixture: signed_off:true with plausible hand-edited signed_off_by=luan and signed_off_at=2026-06-03T00:00:00Z. The structural sign-off envelope check is intentionally permissive of this forgery — it catches accidental hand-stamping (empty/non-ISO-8601 values), not adversarial forgery by an author who knows the envelope shape. Real cryptographic protection is deferred to v2.2. This fixture exists to make the limitation visible.
